claim: Prompt skills instead of single prompts to leverage AI more effectively across repetitive tasks
actionable: Replace ad-hoc prompts with Claude skills that package instructions, tools, and reference files for reusable workflows
source_title: How Anthropic Engineers ACTUALLY Prompt Claude Code

claim: Skills contain three essential layers: description (determines relevance), instructions (step-by-step playbook), and tools (code, scripts, APIs)
actionable: When building a skill, craft a specific description so Claude auto-activates it, write clear sequential instructions, and provide robust tools with good documentation
source_title: How Anthropic Engineers ACTUALLY Prompt Claude Code

claim: Most people obsess over prompt wording and skip the tools layer where the real leverage lives
actionable: Invest effort in building good tools (scripts with clear parameters, API integrations, well-documented functions) rather than only perfecting English prompts
source_title: How Anthropic Engineers ACTUALLY Prompt Claude Code

claim: Build composable, focused skills that can work together rather than one massive skill that does everything
actionable: Split monolithic skills into small, single-purpose skills (e.g., separate YouTube idea research, script writing, and social post drafting) so each can be updated independently
source_title: How Anthropic Engineers ACTUALLY Prompt Claude Code

claim: Save deterministic scripts inside skills as tools to trade cheaper code execution for expensive token generation
actionable: When Claude writes the same Python script repeatedly in a session, save it inside the skill folder so future sessions reuse it instead of regenerating it
source_title: How Anthropic Engineers ACTUALLY Prompt Claude Code

claim: Use user_invocable: false to hide skills from slash menu so only agents can invoke them, and disable_model_invocation: true to restrict high-risk operations to humans only
actionable: Set user_invocable false for internal agent tools and disable_model_invocation true for production deployments or message-sending skills
source_title: How Anthropic Engineers ACTUALLY Prompt Claude Code

claim: Skills improve compounding over time because every session can update the skill with new rules, examples, and edge cases discovered
actionable: After each skill execution, ask Claude to review the session and enhance the skill to handle outputs automatically or prevent the same mistake next time
source_title: How Anthropic Engineers ACTUALLY Prompt Claude Code
